work_unit: PD-TOKEN-01 derived fix — host allowlist + IP-literal block for DEVOUT_SERVER_URL so the deploy token (CODE_PRIVATE_TOKEN/DEVOUT_TOKEN) cannot leak to an arbitrary/misconfigured/DNS-rebinding host. Credential-disclosure / SSRF-adjacent guard on the auth-token-bearing HTTP client path.
situation: audit-2026-07-06-001 (deep audit of prod-deploy scripts/) finding PD-TOKEN-01 (MEDIUM, route-fix): getHeaders() attached X-Agent-Authorization: Code <CODE_PRIVATE_TOKEN> (or token: <DEVOUT_TOKEN>) to every request, to whatever host DEVOUT_SERVER_URL resolved to — no hostname validation, no IP-literal block, no allowlist. A misconfigured/typo'd/DNS-rebinding DEVOUT_SERVER_URL would silently leak the deploy token. Directly analogous to generate-image H02 (audit-2026-07-04-013, merged). This is a derived fix dispatched from fix-in-progress audit cycle; PD-TIME-01 (HIGH) and PD-HANG-01 (MEDIUM) already dispatched+merged on the same opt-prod-deploy-1783270172 worktree in prior rounds.
action: Added host allowlist + IP-literal block to skills/prod-deploy/scripts/lib/http-base.js getBaseUrl(). getBaseUrl() now parses DEVOUT_SERVER_URL with new URL(), strips IPv6 brackets (Node URL.hostname keeps them), and validates hostname against DEFAULT_ALLOWED_HOSTS (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1, *.alibaba-inc.com) plus DEVOUT_ALLOWED_HOSTS env extras (comma-separated, deduped). Disallowed host → throw with a clear message naming the host + allowlist + how to extend. Because every apiGet/apiPost/apiPut calls getBaseUrl() first to build the URL, a throw there prevents getHeaders()/fetch from ever attaching the token to an unverified host. IP literals (IPv4 dotted-quad, IPv6) are rejected unless explicitly exact-allowlisted (blocks 169.254.169.254 metadata-service / internal-IP SSRF). *.alibaba-inc.com suffix rule also matches bare apex (alibaba-inc.com). Single-file fix because PD-HANG-01's refactor (already on this worktree, commit d3a9710) centralized getBaseUrl/getHeaders/apiGet/apiPost/apiPut into http-base.js — the audit's 2-file sketch (task-client + event-client) predates that refactor and now collapses to one file. Verified getHeaders/getBaseUrl have no external importers (only used inside http-base.js api* funcs), so the single gate at getBaseUrl covers all token-bearing call paths. Committed via case-104 escape hatch (direct wt edit + wt-internal git commit) because opt-worktree.sh cp-guard skips files that have diverged on the wt branch (http-base.js diverged: PD-HANG-01 timeout logic is wt-only, not yet on main) — per [[opt-worktree-stack-diverged-escape-hatch]]. Pushed sanctioned auto/opt-prod-deploy-1783270172 branch to origin (auto/ branches are auto-pushed, not main).
direction: route-fix (area=prod-deploy, stacked on existing opt-prod-deploy-1783270172 worktree; no public-interfaces.txt file touched)
outcome_evidence: Behavioral probe (node --input-type=module, import http-base.js): http://localhost:8080 → accepted; http://127.0.0.1:8080 → accepted; https://devout.alibaba-inc.com → accepted; https://sub.devout.alibaba-inc.com → accepted (suffix match); http://[::1]:8080 → accepted (IPv6 loopback, brackets stripped); http://evil.example.com → rejected ('not in the allowlist'); http://10.0.0.5 → rejected (IP literal not allowlisted); http://169.254.169.254 → rejected (AWS metadata IP blocked); http://[2001:db8::1]:8080 → rejected (random IPv6 blocked); DEVOUT_ALLOWED_HOSTS='10.0.0.5, evil.example.com' → both accepted (env extension works); token still attached on allowed host (X-Agent-Authorization: Code <token> verified).

audit_findings.finding: PD-TOKEN-01: getBaseUrl() returned DEVOUT_SERVER_URL with no host validation; getHeaders() attached the deploy token (CODE_PRIVATE_TOKEN/DEVOUT_TOKEN) to every request → token leak to misconfigured/DNS-rebinding host. (Pre-fix state on main: getBaseUrl just stripped trailing slash.)
audit_findings.remediation: Parse URL, validate hostname against allowlist (localhost/127.0.0.1/::1/*.alibaba-inc.com + DEVOUT_ALLOWED_HOSTS env), reject IP literals unless explicitly listed, throw before fetch so token is never sent to unverified host. Implemented in this commit (4fd4f27).

audit_cycle_role: derived-fix (PD-TOKEN-01 dispatched from audit-2026-07-06-001; this case is the fix dispatch, not the audit itself — audit_depth=shallow because the deep audit happened in the parent audit round)
